methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: MinterUpgradeable calculates _emission based on epoch phase (growth/decay/tail)
BLACK minted to MinterUpgradeable if balance insufficient
Team emissions transferred to team address
Rebase transferred to RewardsDistributor
Remaining (_gauge) approved and sent to GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount()
GaugeManager computes index ratio from totalWeight from Voter
GaugeManager._distribute() per gauge computes share from supplyIndex delta * weight
Claimable accumulated per gauge, distributed via IGauge.notifyRewardAmount() or IGaugeCL.notifyRewardAmount()
data_flows.trust_changes: MinterUpgradeable is trusted to produce correct emission. GaugeManager trusts Voter.totalWeight() and Voter.weights() for proportional distribution
exposure_risks: Anyone can call distributeAll/distribute triggering Minter.update_period()
Killed gauge claimable returned to minter but share still computed from global index
manipulation_risks: Voting weight manipulation affects share distribution
Epoch timing attacks to influence when distribution occurs
smNFT balance double-counting in rebase calculation can cause underflow

data_flows.source: Algebra CL Pool trading fees accumulated in CommunityVault
path: GaugeManager._distributeFees() called for CL pool
Reads token balances from CommunityVault
Calls IAlgebraCommunityVault.withdraw() for each token
Tokens sent to gauge (communityFeeReceiver)
IGaugeCL.claimFees() called on gauge
Gauge reads its own token balances
Deducts dibsPercentage and sends to dibs address
Remaining fees approved and sent to internal_bribe via notifyRewardAmount
transformations: Algebra fee deducted by vault during withdraw
sinks: internal_bribe contract (LP fee rewards for voters)
data_flows.trust_changes: GaugeManager calls vault.withdraw directly - requires appropriate permissions on vault. Gauge trusted to correctly compute and forward fees
validation_points: isGauge and isAlive checks before distribution
exposure_risks: GaugeCL uses balanceOf(address(this)) to determine fees - any tokens sent to gauge are treated as fees
GaugeManager may lack permission to call vault.withdraw()
manipulation_risks: Sending tokens directly to gauge address inflates fee amounts
dibsPercentage has no upper bound - can be set to >= 1000 (100%+)
Race between withdraw calls could affect balance reads

path: Briber calls notifyRewardAmount with reward token and amount
Token transferred from briber to Bribe contract
Reward stored in tokenRewardsPerEpoch[token][epochStart]
Voter.vote() calls Bribe.deposit() to record voting weight
At claim time, earned() computes share = balanceOf * tokenRewardsPerEpoch / supply per epoch
GaugeManager.claimBribes() calls Bribe.getReward() which transfers earned to veNFT owner
transformations: Accumulated across all epochs since lastEarn
sinks: AVM original owner if NFT is in AVM
data_flows.trust_changes: Voter is trusted to correctly call deposit/withdraw. GaugeManager trusted as sole caller of getReward
validation_points: isApprovedOrOwner or AVM originalOwner check in claimBribes
exposure_risks: Silent failure in withdraw if amount > balance leads to supply desync
getReward sends to VE owner which could be AVM contract - requires AVM originalOwner lookup
manipulation_risks: Checkpoint manipulation via deposit/withdraw timing within same epoch
Supply checkpoint can be manipulated by large vote deposits right before epoch end
Bribe.withdraw silent failure can desync totalSupply from sum of balanceOf

path: Validate gaugeType < pairFactories.length
Validate pool has no existing gauge
Extract token0, token1 from pool
Validate tokens are whitelisted and at least one is connector
Deploy internal and external bribe contracts via BribeFactory
Deploy gauge via GaugeFactory (standard or CL)
For CL: set gauge as communityFeeReceiver, create eternal farming
Approve MAX uint256 of base token to gauge
Save bribe data, mark gauge alive, push pool to array
transformations: Pool address → gauge, internal_bribe, external_bribe addresses
supplyIndex[gauge] = global index (new gauge starts at current index)
data_flows.trust_changes: No access control on createGauge - anyone can create. Relies on token whitelist and connector checks
exposure_risks: Unlimited approval to newly created gauge
Anyone can create gauges for whitelisted pairs, potentially creating spam
manipulation_risks: Creating gauge for malicious pool that returns controlled token0/token1
New gauge starts at current supplyIndex - misses prior emissions (intended behavior)

data_flows.source: EpochController.performUpkeep() triggered by Chainlink Automation or EPOCH_MANAGER
path: Checks condition via IMinter.check()
Calls genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip()
Calls GaugeManager.distributeAll() which calls Minter.update_period() then distributes to all gauges
Calls GaugeManager.distributeFees() which claims fees for all pools
transformations: Epoch transition triggers emission minting and distribution
data_flows.trust_changes: automationRegistry or EPOCH_MANAGER role can trigger epoch flip
validation_points: update_period has its own epoch boundary check
exposure_risks: genesisManager not set in initialize - reverts if not configured before first upkeep
performPreUpkeep uses automationRegistry2 which is also not initialized
manipulation_risks: EPOCH_MANAGER role holders can trigger epoch flip at will (if condition met)
No MEV protection on distribution ordering

path: GaugeManager iterates nftIds, calls IGaugeCL.getReward(tokenId, isBonusReward)
GaugeCL determines NFT owner via nonfungiblePositionManager.ownerOf(tokenId)
FarmingCenter.collectRewards() collects accrued rewards
FarmingCenter.claimReward() sends reward to NFT owner
transformations: Reward collection from farming virtual pool
data_flows.trust_changes: GaugeManager is DISTRIBUTION, trusted by GaugeCL. No ownership verification of msg.sender for NFT positions
validation_points: NFT owner lookup from position manager
exposure_risks: Anyone can trigger reward claim for any NFT ID - forced claiming
Rewards always go to current NFT owner regardless of who called
manipulation_risks: Grief vector: force-claim rewards for other users at suboptimal times
If isBonusReward flag is wrong, wrong reward type is claimed

core_rules: One gauge per pool - enforced by gauges[_pool] == address(0) check
Emissions distributed proportionally to voting weight per gauge
Gauges can be killed/revived by GOVERNANCE role - killed gauges return claimable to minter
Epoch-based system with WEEK = 1800 seconds (testing config, not production 7 days)
Three emission phases: growth (epochs 1-14, +3%/epoch), decay (epochs 15+, -1%/epoch), tail (weekly < TAIL_START)
Rebase mechanism prevents veNFT holder dilution
Fee distribution: trading fees → communityVault → gauge → dibs deduction → internal bribe
Bribe rewards proportional to voting weight checkpoint at epoch boundaries
economic_incentives: veNFT holders earn bribes by voting on gauges, incentivizing directing emissions to productive pools
LPs earn emissions proportional to gauge allocation from veNFT votes
Bribers deposit tokens to attract votes to specific pools, increasing emissions allocation
Team earns teamRate (up to 5%) of all emissions
Rebase compensates veNFT holders for dilution from new emissions
dibsPercentage referral fee extracted from trading fees before bribe distribution
governance_mechanisms: BlackGovernor allows proposals only targeting Minter.nudge()
Nudge adjusts tailEmissionRate by ±1% based on governance vote outcome
GAUGE_ADMIN role manages factories, bribes, minter configuration
Emergency council can activate/deactivate emergency mode on CL gauges
Quorum based on smNFT past total supply, not overall veNFT supply

edge_cases.actual_behavior: State is updated before event: bribefactory = _bribeFactory; then emit SetBribeFactory(bribefactory, _bribeFactory) emits (newValue, newValue)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Off-chain monitoring and indexing tools will record incorrect old bribe factory address

edge_cases.scenario: GaugeManager._setExternalBribe emits internal_bribes instead of external_bribes as old value
edge_cases.actual_behavior: emit SetBribeFor(false, internal_bribes[_gauge], _external, _gauge) - wrong old value logged
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Off-chain tools and auditing will have incorrect historical records of external bribe changes

edge_cases.scenario: BribeFactoryV3.addRewardsToBribes has inner loop indexing bug
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Inner loop should iterate over _token[i].length for each bribe
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Inner loop iterates k from 0 to _token.length (outer array length) but accesses _token[i][k], causing out-of-bounds if inner arrays are shorter or missing tokens if longer
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Function will revert for most inputs where inner array lengths differ from outer array length, making it unusable. Could skip adding reward tokens to bribes.

edge_cases.scenario: BribeFactoryV3.recoverERC20AndUpdateData calls wrong function on bribe
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should call IBribe.recoverERC20AndUpdateData() to update bribe's tokenRewardsPerEpoch
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Calls IBribe.emergencyRecoverERC20() which does NOT update tokenRewardsPerEpoch, leaving stale reward data
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Bribe contracts will have inflated tokenRewardsPerEpoch after recovery, causing users to claim rewards backed by insufficient token balance, leading to failed transfers for later claimants (insolvency)

edge_cases.scenario: Bribes.withdraw silently fails when amount > balanceOf[tokenId]
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Should revert or handle consistently when amount exceeds balance
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The if(amount <= balanceOf[tokenId]) guard causes silent no-op, but Voter contract likely expects the withdrawal to succeed, creating desync between Voter's tracked weights and Bribe's actual balanceOf/totalSupply
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If Voter calls withdraw with amount > balance (due to rounding or logic error), totalSupply in Bribe remains inflated. This dilutes all voters' bribe rewards since supply used in earned() calculation is higher than actual deposited amounts. Conversely, individual balanceOf entries remain higher, skewing reward distribution.

edge_cases.scenario: MinterUpgradeable.calculate_rebase potential underflow from smNFT double-counting
edge_cases.expected_behavior: circulatingBlack = blackSupply - veBlackSupply should always be non-negative
edge_cases.actual_behavior: veBlackSupply = _veTotal + _smNFTBalance + _superMassiveBonus where _veTotal already includes smNFT locked tokens. Adding _smNFTBalance again double-counts, potentially making veBlackSupply > blackSupply, causing uint underflow revert
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If smNFT balances grow sufficiently, update_period() reverts on calculate_rebase underflow, blocking ALL emission distribution and halting the protocol's epoch progression

edge_cases.expected_behavior: dibsPercentage should be bounded below REFERRAL_FEE_DENOMINATOR (1000)
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any value can be set. If >= 1000, _claimFees will attempt to transfer more than claimed fees, reverting or if unchecked, draining gauge
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious or compromised GAUGE_ADMIN could set referralFee to >= 1000, causing fee distribution to revert (DoS on fee claims) or overflow/underflow in fee calculations

edge_cases.scenario: GaugeFactoryCL.createEternalFarming requires reward tokens not transferred to factory
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Factory should have reward tokens to seed initial farming incentive
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Factory approves 1e10 reward tokens to AlgebraEternalFarming but never receives these tokens. If factory balance < 1e10, addRewards will fail and gauge creation reverts
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: CL gauge creation will fail unless the factory is pre-funded with reward tokens. This is a deployment/operational issue that blocks CL gauge creation entirely.

edge_cases.actual_behavior: require(_int >= address(0)) is always true since every address >= address(0). Internal bribe can be set to address(0), breaking fee distribution
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Owner could accidentally set internal_bribe to address(0), causing _claimFees to revert when trying to approve/notifyRewardAmount on zero address

edge_cases.scenario: GaugeManager.claimRewards for CL gauges has no caller ownership verification
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only NFT owner should be able to trigger reward claims
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any address can call claimRewards(gauge, nftIds, isBonusReward) for any NFT IDs. Rewards are sent to the NFT owner, not the caller
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Grief vector allowing anyone to force-claim rewards for other users at potentially suboptimal times (e.g., tax events, or claiming partial rewards)

edge_cases.expected_behavior: genesisManager should be set during initialization
edge_cases.actual_behavior: initialize() does not set genesisManager. performUpkeep() calls genesisManager.checkAtEpochFlip() which would revert on unset address
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If setGenesisManager is not called before first upkeep, performUpkeep reverts, blocking epoch transitions until configured

edge_cases.scenario: GaugeCL._claimFees uses balanceOf(this) as fee amount
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only trading fees should be counted
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Any tokens sent to gauge address (accidentally or maliciously) are treated as fee income and forwarded to internal_bribe
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Could be used to inject arbitrary amounts into internal bribes, potentially manipulating bribe reward distribution or causing accounting confusion

edge_cases.scenario: GaugeManager gives unlimited approval to each created gauge
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Approval should be bounded to actual distribution amounts
edge_cases.actual_behavior: IERC20(base).approve(_gauge, type(uint256).max) gives each gauge unlimited spending power on GaugeManager's base token balance
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If a gauge contract is compromised or has a vulnerability, it could drain all base tokens from GaugeManager. All gauges share the same approval pool.

edge_cases.scenario: GaugeCL.notifyRewardAmount unsafe uint128 cast of rewardRate
edge_cases.expected_behavior: rewardRate should be validated to fit in uint128
edge_cases.actual_behavior: uint128(rewardRate) silently truncates if rewardRate > type(uint128).max
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Extremely large rewards could cause incorrect rate setting, but practically unlikely with normal token supplies

assumption_violations: WEEK = 1800 (30 minutes) in BlackTimeLibrary - likely testing config. If deployed to production, epoch boundaries are 30 minutes instead of 7 days, breaking all time-based economic assumptions
smNFTBalance is assumed to not be included in ve.balanceOf(ve) but the locked tokens ARE held by the ve contract, leading to double-counting in rebase calculation
GaugeManager assumes it has COMMUNITY_FEE_WITHDRAWER permission on Algebra vault to call withdraw(), but this role is defined but never configured
BribeFactoryV3 assumes Bribe contracts have addReward() function, but provided Bribe contract code does not include this function
GaugeFactoryCL assumes it holds sufficient reward tokens (1e10) for initial farming seeding during gauge creation
game_theory_risks: Voters can concentrate votes at end of epoch after seeing bribe deposits, minimizing their exposure window while maximizing bribe capture
Large veNFT holders can manipulate per-epoch supply checkpoints by timing deposit/withdraw around epoch boundaries to inflate their share of bribe rewards
Forced reward claiming (CL gauges) allows griefing by triggering claims at suboptimal times

Killed gauges still have their weight counted in totalWeight, meaning the killed gauge's emission share is sent back to Minter but reduces others' proportional share - emissions are effectively burned
integration_risks: Algebra CommunityVault integration assumes specific withdraw() access control that may not be granted to GaugeManager
AlgebraEternalFarming.setRates() called from GaugeManager._distribute() requires GaugeManager to have appropriate role on farming contract
Algebra farming incentiveKeys lookup by pool address assumes one-to-one mapping; multiple incentives per pool could cause key mismatch
IAlgebraPool.communityVault() return value assumed stable - if vault is upgraded, gauge fee receiver becomes stale
calculation_errors: GaugeManager.notifyRewardAmount: Math.max(totalWeight, 1) is redundant inside if(totalWeight > 0) block
MinterUpgradeable.calculate_rebase: potential uint underflow in circulatingBlack = blackSupply - veBlackSupply when smNFT balance is double-counted

Bribes.earned: uses getPriorBalanceIndex with _currTs + WEEK - 1 to find last checkpoint in epoch, but if no checkpoint exists in that epoch, it returns the most recent earlier one, which may belong to a prior epoch with different balance

token_semantics.notes: Custom ERC20 with minter-restricted mint. Uses unchecked blocks for recipient balance in _mint and _transfer. No zero-address checks on transfer. transfer() and transferFrom() return bool but use raw _transfer. No EIP-2612 permit. initialMint of 500M tokens (comment says 50M but code mints 500M).
ERC721 NFT representing locked BLACK positions. Supports permanent locks, smNFT (supermassive NFT) with bonus voting power, and AVM delegation.

invariants.name: one_gauge_per_pool
invariants.description: Each pool can have at most one gauge. Enforced by require(gauges[_pool] == address(0x0)) in _createGauge.

invariants.name: supply_conservation_bribes
invariants.description: Bribe totalSupply should equal sum of all balanceOf entries. VIOLATED: withdraw() silently skips if amount > balanceOf, while Voter may track different amounts.

invariants.name: emission_conservation
invariants.description: _emission = _rebase + _teamEmissions + _gauge. Holds as long as calculate_rebase doesn't underflow.

invariants.name: gauge_claimable_accuracy
invariants.description: claimable[gauge] should reflect accumulated emissions proportional to voting weight since last distribution. Killed gauges should have claimable = 0.

invariants.name: epoch_monotonic_progression
invariants.description: active_period advances by WEEK increments. epochCount increments by 1 each update_period call.

invariants.name: black_initial_mint_guard
invariants.description: initialMint can only be called once. Comment says 50M but code mints 500 * 1e6 * 1e18 = 500M tokens.
